pairs.q: A few questions from my side. So firstly, on the revenue part, right? So could you quantify the amount that we have received from Warner in FY '25?
pairs.a: Okay. So whatever Warner do business, we account that in the business. And I think Warner must be in the range of say around 25%.

pairs.q: Okay. So that would be around INR70-odd crores, INR60-odd crores?
pairs.a: Yes, must be.

pairs.q: So then a follow-up on that. So if I look at my 6-monthly balance sheet and compare it to the full year balance sheet, it seems that the collection from Warner in H2 has been a bit lower - as compared to H1. So any specific reason for that?
pairs.a: No, it depends upon many things. Sometimes we have a few deals in advance or payments coming late. So this happens. You can't compare our business as a quarter-to-quarter basis. Maybe Sushant, you can clarify further.

pairs.q: Got it. Okay. And secondly, if I had to kind of get a ballpark figure in terms of what would have been our growth in the YouTube revenue. Do correct me if I'm wrong.Our assumption says that the growth for the full year in YouTube would have been around 14% to 15%. Is that correct? if you could give
pairs.a: Sushant?

pairs.q: Yes. No worries. And last question from my side. So basis your opening commentary, you shared that the content cost for next year would be around 25% to 28% of our revenue. So is the Tips Music Limited April 24, 2025 assumption correct that the absolute amount could be in the range of around INR95 c
pairs.a: Yes, we are targeting that.

pairs.q: Can you help me with the amount of this doubtful debts included in the other expenses?
pairs.a: Sagar, it is around INR2.5 crores

pairs.q: Is there any need for the further provision going further?
pairs.a: No,

pairs.q: Okay. So other expenses would iron out, right, going ahead?
pairs.a: Yes,

pairs.q: Okay. And second question is, as per the last 4 years trend in Q4 quarter, your content cost is typically highest and therefore, the margins in Q4 is lower. So what's the logic for the content cost being highest in Q4?
pairs.a: See, it is not in our control, as and when producers gives us the track and tells us the release date, we have to release the content.

pairs.q: Great set of numbers. Just wanted to understand what's our guidance and what's our outlook for the next year? And how are we looking at it? Is it consistent across what we've been talking about for the last few years?
pairs.a: As you all know, the industry is growing by 15%- 20%. And as our commitment, we will definitely expect we will achieve 30%as that we have achieved inlast few years. And because we have a lot of new releases coming up and a lot of deals already signed, we still target we will achieve that 30%. Plus o

pairs.q: So we are confident of our 30% growth guidance for the next year as well, right? That's what you're saying.
pairs.a: Yes.

pairs.q: Great. Also, you've been mentioning about this flow of premium coming through very sharply. And maybe, Hari ji, you can comment on this. How are we seeing that landscape evolving? Are we getting the benefit of these increased paid subscriptions on Spotify, YouTube? How does that landscape look like?
pairs.a: so every year-on-year basis, the paid subscriptions are increasing. If you see the platforms like Spotify, they are putting in more restrictions and they are pushing the consumers towards paid model. So overall, it's a very promising and a positive thing for the entire industry. That's all I can say

pairs.q: Looking at these collection societies, is that still a growth engine for us? Has that space consolidated further? Or is it going to be similar to what we've seen in the last few years?
pairs.a: No. what we have done is, our publishing deal with SMP is looking very promising. And we expect a lot of growth coming in from that. And yes, IPRS and other societies are continuously growing in India, thanks to the consumption that happens in India.

pairs.q: Okay. Yes. I mean that's what we are betting on, and thanks for that insight. I don't have any other question.
pairs.a: See, as I told you earlier also, please don't look us at a quarter-to-quarter basis. Please see us on a yearly basis. We have achieved our 31% growth and we are targeting ourselves for 30% top line, 30% bottom line, we will grow. So that is our target. And we are working hard towards that, acquiring

pairs.q: Thank you Taurani Ji as well your fantastic result. Our profit margin for whole year is 53.6%, while in quarter 4, it is 39%. So can you guide margin projection for financial year '26 onwards? Tips Music Limited April 24, 2025
pairs.a: See, as I told you earlier also, please don't look us at a quarter-to-quarter basis. Please see us on a yearly basis. We have achieved our 31% growth and we are targeting ourselves for 30% top line, 30% bottom line, we will grow. So that is our target. And we are working hard towards that, acquiring

pairs.q: Two of your songs Taaron Ko Mohabbat Amber Se and Badal Gayi Hai Yeh Duniya, these songs are again famous. So what is the reason for that and has such a thing happened in past also?
pairs.a: See Ravi Ji, this happens, that somebody likes the song and he just made a reel or something and then suddenly, it catches on. So these things happen in our business. There are many Tips songs as I told you our repertoire -- 90s repertoire from '88 till 2020, whatever we have acquired is really doin

pairs.q: Sir, you have shown global music label revenue only 2%. So what is music label at global level? Can you describe this?
pairs.a: Sushant can you take that?

pairs.q: So you mean to say India will grow definitely in comparison to $29 billion, right?
pairs.a: In terms of overall volume-wise, India has a much larger share, but -- its only 2%. That will grow substantially given that subscription is now picking up in India. So that's the underlying message

pairs.q: Okay. And one more thing. We bought less song in this year from 730 songs to443 songs, we paid INR71 crores. In last year, we bought 733 song and paid INR56 crores. So can we treat this now the song cost is costly or what is this?
pairs.a: See, we are acquiring films, and we are focusing on a quality content. As I said earlier in earlier call also, we will be releasing less and less quantity. These are the old songs we have acquired, which we are releasing. But going forward, we are targeting to release 100 -125 songs but we'll invest

pairs.q: Right, right, right. And one last, what about the Bollywood's scene currently going on? Tips Music Limited April 24, 2025
pairs.a: Yes, it's happening. We are looking for a good music. And whenever we have an opportunity, we acquire, good music, we are doing that.

pairs.q: Thanks for the opportunity. I have a few questions. So firstly, on growth, while you have managed to end the year with 29% Y-o-Y growth, this also included benefits to the tune of about INR12 crores in the first half on account of Wynk Music. Excluding the same growth was about 24%. So I understand
pairs.a: See, my content is really doing well and short content are also giving us good money. Streaming is happening. So overall, same drivers, but not a major change. And content plus this year though, we have a lot of good new releases. So I feel we will achieve whatever we are saying. And as far as Wynk

pairs.q: No, no. I mean I understand that, that 24% is also a fairly healthy number. My only question was this ramp up from this 24% to 30% with your Warner numbers already being in the base. But understood, I think it will largely be driven by new content that we acquire, quality -- more quality content com
pairs.a: Yes.

pairs.q: Understood. And secondly, could you explain the working capital movements in this fiscal? So does it pertain to timing differences in receipt of advances from Warner and that the amounts were received in maybe April this time, which is why they are not reflecting in FY '25, something different from
pairs.a: We have received one instalment in March, I feel. And another one we are going to receive in October. So we are receiving whatever -- commitment was there that's happening. Tips Music Limited April 24, 2025

pairs.q: Okay. So this dip in CFO, the reported CFO, that is on account of this very Warner deal?
pairs.a: Kavish, on this Warner deal, what Kumar ji said is one tranche is expected in October. So that's why you see that when you compare it with last year, you will see that dip. Otherwise, there are no other dips on the cash flows.

pairs.q: So in FY '24, the amounts were received, the advances were received in March '24 or April '24?
pairs.a: So, the first tranche we received at the end of March '24.
